knownSurfaces.role: PCI compliance dashboard route referenced by the eleven-labs PCI app
invoice/payment workflow reference in repo docs
billing and subscription pathway referenced by SATCOM docs
knownSurfaces.posture: prefer hosted/payment-link flow; do not store PAN locally
keep separate from cardholder data environment

scope.cardDataAssumption: SATCOM/local systems should not store full PAN, CVV, magnetic-stripe data, PIN data, or payment-card screenshots.

readiness.reason: Static docs and the PCI dashboard route exist, but annual evidence still needs current payment-flow screenshots, processor settings, user access review, scan evidence where applicable, and confirmation that no local workflow stores cardholder data.

controlAreas.expectation: Keep production ports minimal; do not expose internal ports publicly.
controlAreas.evidenceNeeded: Firewall/Plesk/Lightsail screenshot or exported rule summary.

controlAreas.expectation: Harden hosting, admin, and deployment settings.
controlAreas.evidenceNeeded: Server/app config review summary; no default credentials.

controlAreas.expectation: Avoid storing cardholder data in SATCOM/local apps.
controlAreas.evidenceNeeded: Operator statement plus repo/storage search for PAN-like data.

controlAreas.expectation: Protect operator machine and server surfaces.
Keep app dependencies and static deploys reviewed.
controlAreas.evidenceNeeded: Build/test evidence; dependency review for payment app.

controlAreas.expectation: Limit payment/admin access to required users.
Unique accounts and MFA for payment/admin systems.
controlAreas.evidenceNeeded: GitHub, Vercel, Stripe, M365, Plesk MFA/access review.

controlAreas.expectation: Keep physical/payment devices out of scope or documented.
controlAreas.evidenceNeeded: If no card-present devices, record not-applicable rationale.
Payment provider logs and hosting audit log notes.
Vulnerability scan or ASV evidence if required.
This packet plus operator sign-off and hard-review hooks.

hardReviewHooks: payment-card data in prompts, chats, screenshots, CSVs, issue trackers, or source control
live payment, subscription, billing, bank, invoice, or account-access changes
secrets, tokens, private keys, .env, or payment-provider credential handling
DNS, Plesk, SSL, firewall, or domain changes
evidenceChecklist: payment provider PCI/compliance dashboard export or screenshot
confirmation that local systems do not store full PAN/CVV/sensitive authentication data
public HTTPS check for payment-facing domains
privileged access review for Stripe/payment provider, Vercel, GitHub, Plesk, M365, and invoice admin
server/network firewall summary if payment-facing route is self-hosted
dependency/build evidence for PCI dashboard or invoice app
